content.full_text:
19 May 1977

David Johnson  
The Advocate  
1730 Amphlett Suite 225  
San Mateo, CA 94102

Dear Mr. Johnson:

This letter will serve to confirm our telephone conversation of this week in which we discussed what, if any, filing obligation your group would incur as a result of fund raising activity. You stated the following:

Your organization will soon be engaged in various types of activity (direct mail solicitation, concerts, etc.) in an effort to raise funds in support of a measure on the ballot in another state. The solicitation will not imply nor will any of the funds raised actually be used in support or opposition of any measure which may appear on the ballot, as a contribution to any candidate for public office or to an officeholder, or in an attempt to qualify or oppose the qualification of a measure for the ballot in the State of California.

As long as the above information is correct, your organization will have no filing obligations under the Political Reform Act. If, however, your organization at any time acts with a view to influencing the nomination or election of a candidate or the qualification or passage of a measure in California, it will incur certain filing responsibilities.

Please let me know if I may be of additional assistance.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins  
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

RSK/gt

sections.question: What filing obligations, if any, would an organization incur as a result of fund raising activity, specifically direct mail solicitation and concerts, in support of a measure on the ballot in another state, with funds not intended for use in California political activity?
sections.conclusion: As long as the solicitation does not imply and no funds raised are actually used in support or opposition of any measure on the California ballot, as a contribution to any California candidate or officeholder, or in an attempt to qualify or oppose the qualification of a measure for the ballot in California, the organization will have no filing obligations under the Political Reform Act. However, if the organization acts with a view to influencing the nomination or election of a candidate or the qualification or passage of a measure in California, it will incur certain filing responsibilities.
sections.question_synthetic: The organization seeks clarification on whether fund raising activities to support an out-of-state ballot measure would trigger filing obligations under California's Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The organization has no filing obligations under the Political Reform Act as long as funds raised are used only for an out-of-state ballot measure and not for any California political activity. Filing obligations would be triggered if the organization subsequently engages in activity intended to influence California elections or ballot measures.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard confirmation letter following telephone conversation. No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers, but the letter clearly states the factual predicate and the FPPC's legal conclusion. Letter includes conditional language regarding potential future filing obligations.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What filing obligations, if any, would an organization incur as a result of fund raising activity, specifically direct mail solicitation and concerts, in support of a measure on the ballot in another state, with funds not intended for use in California political activity?

CONCLUSION: As long as the solicitation does not imply and no funds raised are actually used in support or opposition of any measure on the California ballot, as a contribution to any California candidate or officeholder, or in an attempt to qualify or oppose the qualification of a measure for the ballot in California, the organization will have no filing obligations under the Political Reform Act. However, if the organization acts with a view to influencing the nomination or election of a candidate or the qualification or passage of a measure in California, it will incur certain filing responsibilities.

The solicitation will not imply nor will any of the funds raised actually be used in support or opposition of any measure which may appear on the ballot, as a contribution to any candidate for public office or to an officeholder, or in an attempt to qualify or oppose the qualification of a measure for the ballot in the State of California. As long as the above information is correct, your organization will have no filing obligations under the Political Reform Act. embedding.summary: FPPC confirms that The Advocate's fund raising activities for an out-of-state ballot measure trigger no California filing obligations, provided the funds are not used for California political activity. The organization must notify FPPC if it subsequently engages in activities intended to influence California elections or measures.
